accept: Quantitative analysis: count commits per closed issue in this repo's history; report the ratio of 'work commits' to 'act-op commits'.
Each option above is evaluated for: implementation cost, audit-trail preservation, multi-writer correctness, behavior under merge/rebase.
Recommendation lands in a docs/ design note (not a CLAUDE.md rule yet) with rationale; v0.2 implements at least the bundling option behind a flag, defaulting to current behavior.
Comparison to beads/doit's commit pattern is documented; if their approach suggests a different design, called out explicitly.
payload.description:
Every act write op is its own commit (claim, close, dep-add, etc.) — for a single 'work an issue end-to-end' loop the typical commit count is 4-5 (claim + work-commit + close + push, plus any deps/follow-ups). In a real production repo's git log this is meaningful noise: 'act-op: (act-XXXX) close' messages dominate the log alongside the actual work commits.

Options to investigate:
1. **Bundling**: a flag (--no-commit already exists, maybe a session-level 'autocommit' default = off + a periodic 'act flush' that bundles N pending ops into one commit) so a single agent's session = one commit.
2. **Separate-branch ops**: keep .act/ ops on a refs/act/ops branch (a la git notes); main stays clean. Tradeoff: harder to inspect, breaks the 'op-log lives in working tree' design.
3. **Squash-on-close**: the close commit incorporates all of the issue's prior op commits as a squashed sequence. Tradeoff: loses individual op timestamps from git log (still in the op file's HLC).
4. **Compaction-driven cleanup**: 'act compact' could rewrite history to drop intermediate op commits once snapshotted. Tradeoff: history rewriting is dangerous.
5. **Status quo + tooling**: agents and humans use 'act log <id>' / git log filters to ignore act-op commits; accept the noise.

Compare to beads' use of doit: beads stores in Dolt (a versioned database), so its 'commits' are different — Dolt commits database transitions, not file changes per op. Beads probably has fewer noisy commits because Dolt batches changes into single-transaction commits. Investigate whether their pattern suggests a model for act.

Recommend: option 1 (bundling with periodic flush) as the default for production use; expose --no-commit + flush as the canonical pattern. Keep current per-op auto-commit as the dev/dogfood default since visibility matters there.
payload.title: investigate + reduce git-log noise from per-op commits
